page.title: lupus nephritis — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for lupus nephritis: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for lupus nephritis: 110 alterations and 235 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Effectiveness and safety of tripterygium glycosides tablet for lupus nephritis.
Cordyceps and artemisinin prevent the recurrence of lupus nephritis.
Eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) may exert beneficial effects on lupus nephritis by decreasing oxidative stress.
Emodin may improve interstitial fibrosis, thus improving prognosis of lupus nephritis.
Flaxseed supplementation appears to be renoprotective in lupus nephritis.
Tripterygium has treatment value in patiens with lupus nephritis.
Turmeric can decrease proteinuria, hematuria, and systolic blood pressure in patients suffering from relapsing or refractory lupus nephritis.
Honokiol suppresses the aberrant interactions between renal resident macrophages and tubular epithelial cells in lupus nephritis.
Luteolin attenuates lupus nephritis by regulating macrophage oxidative stress via HIF-1α pathway.
Protopanaxadiol improves lupus nephritis.
A soy diet accelerates renal damage in mice with lupus.
Antrodia camphorata attenuates the progression of nephritis in systemic lupus erythematosus-prone mice.
Astragalus amplifies the therapeutic effects of cyclophosphamide in the treatmeant of Lupus Nephritis.
Citral alleviates the mouse ASLN model by inhibition of the activation signal of NLRP3 inflammasome and enhanced activation of Nrf2 antioxidant signaling.
Cordyceps protein alleviates renal injury by inhibiting T cell infiltration and Th1 cell differentiation in lupus nephritis mice.
Curcumin attenuates lupus nephritis by inhibiting neutrophil migration.
Effects of Lactobacillus plantarum HFY15 on Lupus nephritis.
Fish oil supplementation and energy restriction protect against renal deterioration and oxidative damage in lupus-prone mice.
Green tea powder inhibits the progression of lupus-like syndrome and prolongs survival in mice.
Hyperoside attenuates Lupus nephritis-associated mesangial cell apoptosis.
Magnolol has a beneficial effect on lupus nephritis.
Oxymatrine ameliorates Lupus nephritis.
Piperine ameliorated lupus nephritis by targeting AMPK-mediated activation of NLRP3 inflammasome.
Procyanidin B2 prevents lupus nephritis development in mice by inhibiting NLRP3 inflammasome activation.
Radix Paeoniae rubra ameliorates lupus nephritis.
SARS-CoV-2 spike aggravates lupus nephritis and lung fibrosis in systemic lupus erythematosus.
The lignan precursor from flaxseed known as secoisolariresinol diglucoside (SDG) may inhibit the progression of lupus nephritis.
The principal active components of the Chinese herbal remedy Tripterygium wilfordii significantly ameliorated lupus nephritis in mice.
Anti-inflammatory and immunoregulatory effects of icariin and icaritin.
Celastrol alleviates murine lupus nephritis via inducting CD4+Foxp3+ regulatory T cells.
Fisetin reduces the senescent tubular epithelial cell burden and also inhibits proliferative fibroblasts in murine lupus nephritis.
Hyperoside ameliorates lupus nephritis by suppressing AKT1-mediated PANoptosis in podocytes.
Paeonol interferes with lupus nephritis by regulating M1/M2 polarization of macrophages.
Punicalagin ameliorates lupus nephritis via inhibition of PAR2.
Quercetin is a potential therapeutic drug for lupus nephritis.
The consequences of altered microbiota in immune-related chronic kidney disease.
Total glucosides of paeony ameliorate pristane-induced Lupus nephritis.
Total glucosides of paeony ameliorates lupus nephritis by suppressing ZBP1-mediated PANoptosis in podocytes.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Spontaneous remission essentially zero in class III/IV LN; progression to ESRD in 10-20% at 10 years on standard therapy; renal flares in 30-50% of patients achieving initial response
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Voclosporin + MMF + low-dose OCS: 40.8% complete renal response at 52 weeks (AURORA 1); belimumab + SOC: 43% primary efficacy renal response (BLISS-LN); obinutuzumab + MMF + SOC: superior histological response vs rituximab (NOBILITY)
remission.gap_size: Moderate — better outcomes available with triple therapy but access and racial disparities limit implementation
remission.barrier_detail: Black and Hispanic patients have significantly worse LN outcomes and are underrepresented in landmark trials; voclosporin and belimumab add significant cost; cyclophosphamide still used in some settings where MMF is equally effective in non-Black patients (Euro-Lupus low-dose equivalent); racial disparities in specialist access
remission.last_soc_change: 2021 — voclosporin (Lupkynis) approved; belimumab IV added for LN (BLISS-LN); MMF established as standard for induction since ACR 2012 guidelines; cyclophosphamide from 1986 (NIH protocol)
remission.outdated_treatments: High-dose cyclophosphamide (original NIH protocol) for all LN (Euro-Lupus low-dose equivalent efficacy with less toxicity); azathioprine for induction in active LN (maintenance only; inferior to MMF for induction); OCS monotherapy for mild class II LN without hydroxychloroquine
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Hydroxychloroquine optimised dosing (renal-protective independent of disease activity — weight-based dosing 5mg/kg preferred); vitamin D optimisation; dietary: low-sodium for hypertension management in LN; ACEI/ARB for proteinuria reduction
remission.frontier_signal: CAR-T for SLE/LN (German case series: drug-free remission achieved); obinutuzumab (anti-CD20 glycoengineered — Phase 3 NOBILITY data positive); dapirolizumab pegol (anti-CD40L); anifrolumab extension to LN (Phase 2 TULIP-LN)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
